results.description: STEP-01: explicit functional scope — `in` non-empty AND `out` present (in/out scope)
STEP-01: open questions listed (array present, even if empty when the brief is clear)

besoins: B01 — Self-care area: B2B customers (brokers, corporate clients) can manage their account, contacts, delegations and documents autonomously without calling the insurer (functional)
B02 — Policy tracking: real-time consultation of contract portfolio, guarantees, endorsements, premium schedules and payment status (functional)
B03 — Claims filing: end-to-end digital FNOL (first notice of loss) with supporting-document upload, status tracking and notifications until settlement (functional)
B04 — Omnichannel access: responsive web portal + mobile app (iOS/Android) with feature parity on core journeys (functional)
B05 — Secure authentication and habilitation model: SSO, MFA, role-based access per company / subsidiary / broker delegation (non-functional — security)
B06 — Integration with core insurance back-ends: policy administration system, claims management system, billing, CRM and EDM/GED (functional — integration)
B07 — Compliance: GDPR, insurance-sector regulation (DDA/IDD, ACPR requirements), accessibility (RGAA/WCAG) (non-functional — compliance)
B08 — Performance and availability: SLA suited to B2B usage (target ≥ 99.5%), response time < 2s on key screens (non-functional — performance)
B09 — Migration and decommissioning of the legacy portal: data recovery, account migration, no service interruption (functional — transition)
B10 — Reporting and self-service extracts for corporate clients: loss ratios, claims history, downloadable statements (functional)
B11 — Change management: communication, training and support for B2B customers and internal teams during the switchover (organizational)
partiesPrenantes: Business sponsor — Head of B2B / Commercial Insurance (project owner, funds and arbitrates)
B2B customers — brokers, agents, corporate risk managers, SME clients (end users of the portal)
Business departments — underwriting/policy management, claims handling, billing/collection (process owners)
MOA/AMOA — Business Analyst, MOA project manager (requirements, UAT, change management)
IT / MOE — CIO, IT architects, dev teams web & mobile, integration team (delivery)
DPO & Legal/Compliance — GDPR, IDD/DDA, contractual terms (regulatory validation)
CISO / Security — habilitation model, MFA, pentests (security validation)
Customer service / B2B support — level-1 support, impacted by self-care shift
UX/UI design team — journeys, design system, accessibility
Data team — reporting, KPIs, migration data quality
External vendors/integrators — software vendor or agency if build is outsourced
Governance committees — COMEX/COSTRAT (strategic arbitration), COPIL (steering), COTECH (technical), CCB (change control)

in: Rebuild of the B2B customer portal (web responsive) and native/hybrid mobile app
Self-care area: account management, users, delegations, documents, notifications
Policy tracking: portfolio view, guarantees, endorsements, premium schedule, payment status
Claims filing and tracking: FNOL forms per line of business, document upload, status workflow, alerts
Authentication, SSO, MFA and B2B habilitation model (multi-entity, broker delegations)
Integrations (API) with policy admin, claims, billing, CRM and EDM systems
Migration of existing B2B accounts and history from the legacy portal
Self-service reporting for B2B clients (claims history, statements, extracts)
UAT (MOA acceptance), accessibility and security compliance
Change management and training for B2B customers and internal support teams
Decommissioning of the legacy B2B portal
out: B2C retail customer portal and journeys
Rebuild or replacement of core back-end systems (policy admin, claims engine, billing)
New insurance product design, pricing or underwriting rules
Internal employee tools (back-office workstations), except APIs exposed to the portal
Broker extranet commissioning/remuneration module (unless confirmed at scoping)
Native offline mode and non-iOS/Android platforms

questionsOuvertes: Who exactly are the B2B users: brokers only, corporate clients directly, or both — and with which delegation model?
Which lines of business (P&C, health, fleet, liability…) are in scope for claims filing at go-live vs later releases?
Is this a full rebuild (rewrite) or a re-platforming on a vendor solution (buy vs build)?
What are the target back-end systems and their API maturity (real-time vs batch integration)?
Mobile strategy: native apps, hybrid (React Native/Flutter), or PWA — and is feature parity mandatory at V1?
What is the migration strategy for the legacy portal: big bang or progressive by customer segment, and what is the coexistence period?
What budget envelope, target go-live date and PRINCE2 tolerances are set by the sponsor?
Which KPIs define success: self-care adoption rate, call deflection, NPS B2B, claims cycle time?
Are electronic signature and online premium payment required in V1?
What SLA and support model is contractually promised to B2B customers?

results.description: STEP-03: backlog of 8 to 15 User Stories
STEP-03: each US carries statement + priorite + estimation (number) + dod
STEP-03: each US follows the « As a … I want … so that … » template

epics: EPIC-01 — Self-care Account & Access Management: B2B account administration, users, broker delegations, documents and secure authentication (SSO/MFA, multi-entity habilitation model) — covers B01, B05
EPIC-02 — Policy Portfolio Tracking: real-time consultation of contracts, guarantees, endorsements, premium schedules, payment status and self-service reporting/extracts — covers B02, B10
EPIC-03 — Digital Claims (FNOL to Settlement): end-to-end claims filing per line of business, supporting-document upload, status workflow and notifications — covers B03

EPIC-04 — Omnichannel Platform & Back-end Integration: responsive web portal + iOS/Android app with core-journey parity, API integration with policy admin/claims/billing/CRM/EDM, performance & availability SLA, GDPR/IDD/RGAA compliance — covers B04, B06, B07, B08

EPIC-05 — Legacy Migration & Adoption: migration of B2B accounts and history, decommissioning of the legacy portal, change management, training and support for customers and internal teams — covers B09, B11

backlog.statement: As a B2B customer (broker or corporate risk manager), I want to authenticate via SSO with MFA and access only the entities and delegations granted to my role, so that my company's insurance data remains secure and segregated per subsidiary/broker mandate.
backlog.dod: SSO + MFA operational on web and mobile; role-based habilitation matrix (company/subsidiary/delegation) implemented and unit-tested; pentest passed with no critical/high finding; CISO sign-off; GDPR access-logging active; automated tests green in CI; deployed to staging and validated by PO.

backlog.statement: As a B2B account administrator, I want to create, modify and deactivate user accounts and broker delegations for my organization autonomously, so that I can manage access without calling the insurer's support.
backlog.dod: CRUD on users and delegations available in self-care area; audit trail of all habilitation changes; email notifications sent on account events; acceptance tests (Gherkin) passed; accessibility RGAA AA checked on the journey; documentation updated; PO acceptance in Sprint Review.

backlog.statement: As a corporate client, I want to view my full contract portfolio with guarantees, endorsements, premium schedules and payment status in real time, so that I can steer my insurance coverage without contacting my account manager.
backlog.dod: Portfolio screen fed by real-time API from the policy administration and billing systems; data accuracy validated against back-end on a UAT sample; response time < 2s on the portfolio screen (P95); responsive design validated mobile/desktop; automated integration tests green; PO acceptance.

backlog.statement: As a broker, I want to file a claim (FNOL) online for my client with a guided form adapted to the line of business, so that the claim is registered immediately and settlement starts faster.
backlog.dod: FNOL form per in-scope line of business connected to the claims management system via API; mandatory-field validation and error handling implemented; claim reference returned in real time; Gherkin acceptance tests passed; RGAA accessibility checked; end-to-end demo in Sprint Review; PO acceptance.

backlog.statement: As a claimant B2B customer, I want to upload supporting documents and track my claim status with notifications at each step until settlement, so that I know where my claim stands without calling support.
backlog.dod: Document upload integrated with the EDM/GED system (formats, size limits, antivirus scan); status timeline synchronized with the claims workflow; email/push notifications triggered on status changes; automated tests green; GDPR retention rules applied to documents; PO acceptance.

backlog.statement: As a B2B customer on the move, I want to access the core journeys (portfolio view, claim filing, claim tracking) from the iOS/Android mobile app, so that I can act on my insurance matters anywhere with the same capabilities as the web portal.
backlog.dod: Core journeys available on iOS and Android with functional parity vs web; app published to internal test tracks (TestFlight/Play Console); crash-free rate ≥ 99% on test cohort; mobile-specific security review passed; store guidelines compliance checked; PO acceptance on both platforms.

backlog.statement: As a corporate risk manager, I want to download self-service reports (loss ratios, claims history, statements), so that I can analyze my risk profile and prepare renewals without requesting extracts from the insurer.
backlog.dod: Report screens and CSV/PDF export available; figures reconciled with the Data team against source systems on a UAT dataset; export performance validated on large portfolios; GDPR minimization respected in extracts; acceptance tests passed; PO acceptance.

backlog.statement: As a self-care user, I want to manage my company profile, contacts and contractual documents in a dedicated account area, so that my organization's data stays up to date without back-office intervention.
backlog.dod: Account area with editable profile/contacts synchronized to the CRM via API; contractual documents retrieved from the EDM; changes propagated to back-ends and audit-trailed; Gherkin acceptance tests passed; RGAA AA checked; PO acceptance.

backlog.statement: As an existing legacy-portal user, I want my account, delegations and history to be migrated automatically to the new portal, so that I can switch without re-registering or losing any data.
backlog.dod: Migration scripts executed and reconciled (100% account count match, sampled data-quality checks with the Data team); rollback procedure tested; zero service interruption during switchover verified; migration dry-run signed off in pre-production; COPIL go decision documented; PO acceptance.

backlog.statement: As the Head of B2B (sponsor), I want the portal to meet GDPR, IDD/DDA and RGAA/WCAG requirements with evidence, so that the company avoids regulatory sanctions and serves all B2B users inclusively.
backlog.dod: GDPR register updated and DPO sign-off obtained; consent and data-retention rules implemented; RGAA audit ≥ AA on core journeys with remediation of blocking issues; legal/compliance validation recorded; compliance evidence attached to the release note.

backlog.statement: As a B2B support agent, I want training materials and an assisted-onboarding kit for the new portal, so that I can guide customers during the switchover and absorb the self-care transition.
backlog.dod: Training kit (guides, FAQ, videos) published on Confluence; at least one training session delivered to level-1 support with attendance recorded; customer communication plan validated by the sponsor; support scripts updated; feedback loop set up for the first 2 sprints post-launch.

backlog.statement: As a Product Owner, I want real-time API integrations with the policy admin, claims, billing, CRM and EDM back-ends monitored with health checks and alerting, so that the ≥ 99.5% availability SLA and < 2s response-time targets are met and measurable.
backlog.dod: API contracts documented and versioned; monitoring dashboard (availability, latency P95, error rate) live; alerting thresholds configured; load test passed at target volumetry; degraded-mode behavior defined and tested; COTECH validation; PO acceptance.

backlog.statement: As the Head of B2B (sponsor), I want the legacy B2B portal decommissioned after a validated coexistence period, so that we eliminate double run costs and security exposure of the obsolete platform.
backlog.dod: Adoption threshold on the new portal reached and evidenced (usage KPIs); redirection from legacy URLs in place; legacy data archived per retention policy; legacy infrastructure shut down; decommissioning report validated in COPIL.

results.description: STEP-04: at least one Gherkin scenario produced
STEP-04: each scenario carries given + when + then (non-empty)
STEP-04: coverage beyond the nominal — at least one `error` case and one `boundary` case

gherkin.given: Given a broker with an active account, MFA enrolled, and a delegation granted on subsidiary "ACME-FR" only
gherkin.when: When he authenticates via SSO and completes the MFA challenge
gherkin.then: Then he accesses the portal, sees only the contracts and claims of "ACME-FR", and the access is written to the GDPR access log

gherkin.given: Given a user whose broker delegation on subsidiary "ACME-DE" has been revoked by the B2B account administrator
gherkin.when: When he attempts to open a policy belonging to "ACME-DE" via a direct URL (forced browsing)
gherkin.then: Then the portal returns a 403 access-denied response, no policy data is exposed, and the denied attempt is audit-trailed

gherkin.given: Given a user who has failed the MFA challenge 4 times, with an account-lockout threshold set to 5 attempts
gherkin.when: When he submits a 5th consecutive invalid MFA code
gherkin.then: Then the account is locked, a security notification email is sent, and a 6th attempt is rejected even with a valid code until unlock

gherkin.given: Given a B2B account administrator authenticated on the self-care area
gherkin.when: When he creates a new user with the "Risk Manager" role scoped to two subsidiaries and confirms
gherkin.then: Then the user account is created, an activation email is sent, and the habilitation change appears in the audit trail with author, timestamp and scope

gherkin.given: Given a B2B account administrator creating a user account
gherkin.when: When he submits the form with an already-registered email address
gherkin.then: Then the creation is rejected with an explicit non-blocking inline error message, no duplicate account is created, and no notification email is sent

gherkin.given: Given a corporate client with 120 active contracts across 3 subsidiaries and up-to-date billing data in the back-end
gherkin.when: When he opens the portfolio screen
gherkin.then: Then contracts, guarantees, endorsements, premium schedules and payment statuses are displayed consistently with the policy-admin and billing systems, with P95 response time under 2 seconds

gherkin.given: Given a corporate client whose portfolio contains exactly one contract expiring today at 23:59
gherkin.when: When he consults the portfolio at 23:58 and again at 00:01 the next day
gherkin.then: Then the contract is shown as "active" before expiry and as "expired" after, with no ghost guarantee remaining displayed

gherkin.given: Given a broker authenticated with a valid delegation for his client and the claims back-end API available
gherkin.when: When he completes the guided FNOL form for the "Property" line of business with all mandatory fields and submits
gherkin.then: Then the claim is registered in the claims management system, a claim reference is returned in real time, and a confirmation notification is sent

gherkin.given: Given a broker filing an FNOL while the claims back-end API is unavailable
gherkin.when: When he submits a valid FNOL form
gherkin.then: Then the portal displays the defined degraded-mode message, no data is silently lost, the submission is retriable, and the incident is visible on the monitoring dashboard with alerting triggered

gherkin.given: Given a claimant uploading supporting documents with a per-file size limit of 10 MB and allowed formats PDF/JPG/PNG
gherkin.when: When he uploads one PDF of exactly 10 MB and one PDF of 10 MB + 1 byte
gherkin.then: Then the 10 MB file is accepted, antivirus-scanned and stored in the EDM, while the oversized file is rejected with an explicit message and no partial upload remains

gherkin.given: Given a claimant uploading a supporting document infected with the EICAR test signature
gherkin.when: When the antivirus scan runs on the uploaded file
gherkin.then: Then the file is quarantined and not stored in the EDM, the user is informed of the rejection, and the event is logged for security review

gherkin.given: Given a claim whose status changes from "Under review" to "Settled" in the claims workflow
gherkin.when: When the status-change event is propagated to the portal
gherkin.then: Then the claim timeline is updated in real time and an email/push notification is delivered to the subscribed claimant

gherkin.given: Given a legacy-portal user with the maximum observed history volume (10 years of claims, 250 delegations) selected in the migration dry-run sample
gherkin.when: When the migration scripts run and the reconciliation report is produced
gherkin.then: Then 100% of accounts are matched, sampled data-quality checks pass, delegations and history are intact on the new portal, and the tested rollback procedure remains executable

gherkin.given: Given the core journeys (portfolio, FNOL, claim tracking) deployed on the iOS and Android test tracks
gherkin.when: When the same end-to-end journey is executed on mobile and on the web portal
gherkin.then: Then functional parity is verified, the crash-free rate stays ≥ 99% on the test cohort, and screen-reader navigation passes the RGAA AA checks on the journey

output.planTest:
AGILE TEST PLAN — B2B Self-care Insurance Portal (EPIC-01 to EPIC-05)

1. TEST STRATEGY (shift-left, in-sprint). Testing starts at backlog refinement: each Must Have story gets ATDD acceptance criteria written as Gherkin (Given/When/Then) in Three Amigos sessions (PO + Dev + QA) BEFORE development. All tests execute within the sprint; the story is only Done when its DoD and the team DoD are met.

2. TEST PYRAMID & AUTOMATION. Base: unit tests (habilitation matrix, field validation, premium calculations) — dev-owned, TDD, run on every commit. Middle: API/integration tests (policy admin, claims, billing, CRM, EDM contracts; contract testing on versioned API specs; degraded-mode and error-handling paths) — automated, run in CI per merge. Top: thin E2E UI layer (Cucumber/Playwright) limited to the core journeys: SSO/MFA login, portfolio consultation, FNOL, document upload, claim tracking. Mobile: parity suite executed on iOS/Android test tracks (TestFlight/Play Console).

3. TEST TYPES & OWNERSHIP. (a) AUTOMATED: regression suite in CI/CD — full pyramid on every pipeline run, blocking quality gate before staging deploy. (b) MANUAL: scripted acceptance validation of Gherkin scenarios in the sprint, RGAA AA accessibility checks on each new journey, migration reconciliation review with the Data team. (c) EXPLORATORY: session-based testing (60–90 min charters) each sprint on fresh increments — priority charters: habilitation segregation (forced browsing across subsidiaries/delegations), FNOL edge cases per line of business, upload robustness (formats, size boundaries, antivirus), mobile/web parity gaps.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL (in-sprint, not deferred). Performance: load test at target volumetry, P95 < 2s on portfolio screen, SLA ≥ 99.5% verified via monitoring dashboard (availability, latency P95, error rate) and alerting thresholds. Security: MFA lockout, session handling, access-log GDPR compliance every sprint; pentest with zero critical/high before EPIC-01 release; mobile security review before store publication. Compliance: GDPR (consent, retention on claim documents, minimization in exports), IDD evidence, RGAA ≥ AA audit on core journeys — compliance evidence attached to each release note.

5. MIGRATION TESTING (EPIC-05). Dry-run in pre-production: 100% account-count reconciliation, sampled data-quality checks, rollback rehearsal, zero-interruption switchover verification — exit criterion for the COPIL go decision.

6. ENTRY/EXIT CRITERIA. Entry: story refined with Gherkin AC, testable in sprint, test data and stubbed/live back-ends available. Exit (per story DoD): all Gherkin acceptance tests green, automated tests in CI green, no open blocking/major defect, accessibility checked, PO acceptance in Sprint Review.

7. QUALITY METRICS & IMPROVEMENT. Tracked per sprint: automated coverage per pyramid level, in-sprint defect escape rate, regression suite duration and flakiness, crash-free rate (mobile), P95 latency trend, accessibility non-conformities. Reviewed in a QA retrospective each sprint; corrective actions enter the next sprint backlog.

8. RISKS & MITIGATION. Back-end API instability → contract tests + service virtualization for in-sprint testing. Habilitation regression (highest business risk) → dedicated automated segregation suite run on every build. Migration data loss → reconciliation gates + tested rollback. Late accessibility findings → RGAA checks embedded in each story's DoD, not batched at the end.
